text:
Athens Times

—

It has been a month since he stole the performance during the Paris Olympic Games ceremony and people are still talking about it. Tom Cruz, wanted to “enter the shoes” of Ethan Hunt, the hero who plays in the “Mission Impossible” films, and left an era with their daring appearance in closing the curtain of the Olympic Games. The wild Hollywood star jumped from the top of the stadium to take the Olympic flag and climbed on a machine with which the journey to Les Angeles began. With a vino that has been filmed on both sides of the Atlantic, the leading protagonist seems to be passing through the Paris immersive flag, falling with undefeated to reach Hollywood, before giving in turn the Olympic flag to other famous athletes, Kate Courtney, Michael Johnson, Jacker Eaton. As revealed on CNBC panels X Boardroom Casey Vazerman, president of the Olympic and Paralympic Games Los Angeles 2028, Cruz refused to take a cent for his performances – for live in the stadium and the recorded place. As he said, he spoke with him via Zoom to discuss the idea, and it quickly became clear that not only was he ready for this particular “roll”, but he wanted to do all his own tricks, just as he does in “Mission: Impossible” Not only did he dive into the whole project, but Wasserman explained that Cruise – and all those involved in magnetized shooting – did it all for free. “He completed filming “Mission Impossible” at 6 p.m. in London, got directly on the plane. He landed in Los Angeles at 4 a.m. and filmed the scene where he was on a military plane. In Los Angeles, he makes two jumps from the plane. He didn’t like the first one, so he made a second jump. Then he went by helicopter from Palmdale to the Hollywood sign, turned from 1 to 5, went by helicopter to Burbank Airport and flew back to London,” he said features.

Athens Times

News about Greece and the World.
